cards.front: What is Project Management Institute (PMI) Time Estimation Model?
cards.back: A method of estimating time required for a project task, developed by the Project Management Institute (PMI)

cards.front: What is the Three-Point Estimate method?
cards.back: A method of estimating project duration, which consists of a most likely, best, and worst-case estimate

cards.front: What is the PDPC (Program Development Planning Control) Approach?
cards.back: A method of estimating time required for a project task, which considers the past performance of similar tasks

cards.front: What is the Parametric Estimation Model?
cards.back: A method of estimating project duration, which uses historical data and mathematical formulas to estimate the time required for a project task

cards.front: What is the Expert Judgment Estimation Model?
cards.back: A method of estimating project duration, which relies on the experience and expertise of a team of specialists or experts
